format_discipline.first_line: # High-stakes domains where AI must not have the final word — medical, legal, safety

locations: three named domains EU AI Act

resolved_corpus_soft_findings: MRI expanded: 'MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging)' on first use
CT expanded: 'CT (computed tomography)' on first use

abstract_first.note: Three-signal table follows concrete film-vs-cancer-treatment example. COMPAS introduced with full name before use.

unlisted_sequence.note: Four arguments as numbered list. Four meaningful-oversight conditions as numbered list. Worked Example steps as bold-header walkthrough.

no_inline_examples.note: Concrete examples inline throughout: film vs cancer treatment, radiologist scenario, NHS mammography, COMPAS, Boeing 737 MAX.

bold_term_shape.note: high-stakes domain, decision support, meaningful, safety-critical, high-risk AI, XAI all bolded at first use.

comparison_table.note: Three-signal table in Key Concepts. Failure/lesson/fix table in In Practice.

scope_discipline.topic_title: High-stakes domains where AI must not have the final word — medical, legal, safety

sampled_assertions.claim: three signals: irreversibility, fundamental interests, asymmetric error cost
EU AI Act 2024 classifies three domains as high-risk
COMPAS produced systematically different error rates across demographic groups (ProPublica 2016)
MCAS nose-down from faulty sensor, 346 deaths
four arguments: cost-of-error, accountability, explainability, cognitive bias
meaningful oversight: time + data + training + easy override
NHS mammography AI pre-screener, radiologist as authority
Boeing 737 MAX regulatory changes: pilot training, simplified override, enhanced alerting

diagram_embedded.location: Key Concepts, after three-named-domains intro paragraph
diagram_embedded.alt_text: High-stakes domains and the human final authority principle
